summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #616 where the exec tool ignores `default_timeout_secs` and `max_output_bytes` config values from `[tools.exec]`, causing hardcoded defaults to always be used.
summary.outcome: Added two builder methods (`with_default_timeout`, `with_max_output_bytes`) to `ExecTool`, wired them to config values in `server.rs`, added 4 passing tests, and committed the fix with all 40 exec tests passing.

repo: ExecTool uses a builder pattern with `with_*` methods; 7 such methods already existed before this change.
`max_output_bytes` was already wired for remote/node execution (server.rs ~line 3547) but not for local execution — this fix brings local execution to parity.
Config fields for exec tool (`default_timeout_secs`, `max_output_bytes`) already existed and were validated in schema.rs/validate.rs; only the wiring was missing.
server.rs uses fully-qualified `std::time::Duration` rather than importing it at the top of the file.
There is a known pre-existing build failure for `llama-cpp-sys-2` CMake on macOS (tracked as `moltis-csq`) that appears in clippy/full builds but is unrelated to this change.
The project uses a 'beads' issue tracker alongside GitHub issues; a beads issue was created and closed as part of the workflow.

code.finding: Added `with_default_timeout(Duration)` and `with_max_output_bytes(usize)` builder methods following existing `with_*` pattern.
ExecTool construction site where config values must be chained in; `max_output_bytes` for remote nodes is wired ~line 3547.

workflow: Running clippy on only the changed crates (`crates/tools` and `crates/gateway`) is faster and sufficient to verify new code is clean when a full build has a pre-existing unrelated failure.
Formatting (rustfmt) should be verified before committing; a pre-existing diff in `providers/src/lib.rs` was distinguished from the new change's formatting issue.

friction: `Duration` was not imported in server.rs and required a second edit to switch to fully-qualified `std::time::Duration` after initially using the bare type.
Full clippy run triggered the pre-existing `llama-cpp-sys-2` CMake build failure on macOS, requiring a targeted per-crate clippy run to verify the actual changes.
A rustfmt diff appeared in server.rs for the new line and required a separate fix edit before formatting passed.
open_items: The pre-existing `llama-cpp-sys-2` CMake build failure on macOS (tracked as `moltis-csq`) was noted but not addressed.
Pre-existing rustfmt issues in `providers/src/lib.rs` were not fixed as they were out of scope.
